text: Handling deadlines effectively is a key skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps, allowing you to work steadily without last-minute panic. Prioritization is equally important—focus on urgent or high-value tasks first, and leave less critical work for later. Scope management means understanding what is realistic to achieve within the time given. If your schedule becomes too full, avoid overcommitting; instead, adjust your goals or ask for more time. Finally, clear communication with colleagues or supervisors about progress or problems can prevent misunderstandings and lead to better results. With these strategies, deadlines become less stressful and more manageable.
